## [00:00 - 03:07]

What's going on guys in this video, we're going to be talking about how you can start using creative content to really start helping you get a lot more sales. Now real quick, I want to tell a story about how I actually came about shooting creative content for my drop-tripping stores. This year in February, I launched a new store, I scaled it to about $2,000 a day within the first month and I got satisfied. You know, I got comfortable, something that all successful people say not to do. And during that time, I had a competitor come in, it sadly was my friend who saw the product on my store, you know, that's just part of the game. And he pretty much started selling that product and making $10,000 a day with the same product, same type of ads that I was running. So it made me take a hard look at my Facebook because all of my CPMs, all of my click-through rates, cost per clicks, my cost per purchases most importantly, you know, they all went sideways. And so I started losing money and this really made me tweak my business and really pivot. And so this time I decided I was going to hire some models, they were actually some model friends of mine and they were going to start helping me shoot content, wearing my product and using my product. So right around that time was actually Coachella, I was out there, they came out there, we're having a good time and we just shot a quick video literally within five minutes. The next day I put it on, put it up on Facebook, I spent $120 on ads and made $1,400 back in sales. And right away, like all my Facebook CPMs, everything was so much better and I scaled up within a few days to $10,000 a day. And so it really showed me that this was critical in any dropshipping business of mine. And I really think a lot of dropshippers are missing out on creating their own content for their ads just because the business model of this itself is dropshipping. So you don't really have the product, right? All the products are coming from your manufacturers and your warehouses. So you guys get lazy, I've done it in the past, I know, and you just use the product images that your suppliers give you. So from now on out, I want you guys to test with those photos, that's fine, test with the photos, but if your ads start picking up, 100% you need to order the products to yourself and start shooting ads with them. You don't need anything crazy, you don't need any crazy cameras, just a basic iPhone and smartphone will be perfect for this. And then I'm also going to show you guys a few apps that you guys can pretty much just start editing all of your video clips with. And these are apps that Facebook actually recommends. Again, well, me and Jeremy were up there at their headquarters, they gave us these apps and said, literally like this is what we want on Facebook. So just use them. And when you do, they benefit you. You can download it on the App Store or Google Play. You can add in any text you want. You can add in music, animations, all sorts of stuff for either swipe up videos or just regular Facebook and Instagram posts.

## [03:07 - 06:16]

So this one's super critical. This is pretty much where I edit all of my videos now after I shoot them. And then another app that you can use, excuse me, use, especially in the beginning, if you do want to test with just the product photos, it's called Legend. It turns product photos kind of into video animations with text over them. So it's definitely something you should use in the beginning if you don't have the products, but you want to test the video instead of just photos. And something you guys will notice when you do test videos, all of your Facebook CPMs will be much lower just because Facebook loves videos on their platform. And something to really know here when you're creating these videos is try to make it entertaining. Don't be super, super salesy. Facebook doesn't like that. They want people to be on their platform and join it going through all the posts. So if you're just always like sell, sell, sell, spam, you know, Facebook really doesn't like that. Their algorithm is just going to push you farther down. So make sure you create something that's kind of fun and creative and isn't too pushy. And I'm going to give you guys a perfect example. We're going to hop right back in here into my business manager. And you guys can see right here. This was an old video I created for this backpack. It was this cool light up backpack. I'm going to play the video real quick for you guys. And when I launched this, it's funny. I actually shot this with my mom, which is pretty funny. I was like, mom, listen, I need a model and I'm here at your house and I have this backpack. So we're just going to make a really good looking ad with you, you know, putting it on and using it. And we did and the video went viral. So it's pretty cool. You can see I have over, you know, 200 shares just within the first couple of days of running this, a bunch of likes, comments, people tagging their friends. And that's what Facebook wants. And when you give Facebook what they want, they're going to reward you. You're going to get much better results, cheaper costs, per purchases, everything. And this is why really creating content for yourself is super critical. And not only that, not only for Facebook, it's very critical. And it sets you aside from all of your competitors. Just think of it now. I mean, dropshipping is very saturated. Me, you, so many other people are trying to sell the same exact products every day. And so think about that. If we're all using the same photos that our vendors are giving us, well, it's pretty much just comes down to who can really start selling it first and scale their Facebook ads first. But when you go out there and you create content, you could create content for a product that's already very saturated, but you can really tailor it around and brand it differently. And people will start coming to your store and buying. And then you can also shoot photos for your store, you know, videos for your store and start branding your entire business differently when you start creating your own content for dropshipping. So again, guys, make sure you order these products from your vendors and start shooting content with them.
